name: Property Dispute Resolution Checklist
description: Action steps for resolving property disputes and boundaries

details: Collect property deed, title documents, registration certificate
Get survey reports and boundary demarcation documents
Collect all purchase/sale agreements and previous ownership records
Get municipal property tax records and utility bills
Compile all correspondence related to the property
Verify property registration with Revenue Department
Check for any encumbrances, mortgages, or legal claims
Obtain certified copy of registered document
Check if property is under dispute or litigation
Get title insurance if available
Hire lawyer specializing in property disputes and conveyancing
Discuss legal merits of your claim
Understand applicable real estate and property laws
Discuss settlement vs litigation options
Estimate case timeline and costs
Send formal notice proposing settlement to other party
Meet for negotiation (with lawyer if needed)
Explore mediation or arbitration options
If settlement reached, document in written agreement
Get agreement registered if required

base_steps.action: File civil suit if settlement fails
details: File suit for possession, declaration of title, or boundaries
Pay court filing fees and stamp duty
Serve summons to other party
File detailed plaint with all evidence
Appear before court for preliminary hearings
Collect witness statements from neighbors and previous owners
Get expert reports on boundary, construction, damages
Compile photographs/videos of property and boundaries
Get municipal/government records and maps
Prepare chronological timeline of dispute events

India.action: Apply for injunction to prevent unauthorized use
details: Request court injunction to prevent other party from using property
Apply for interim injunction if property is being misused
Request compensation for trespass or unauthorized occupation
Monitor injunction compliance
